scenario.title: Asymmetric Disaggregation & Merger
scenario.description: Models a complex 'fracture'. Legacy Council A is primarily merging with Council B to form 'Unitary AB'. However, border boundary changes mean Council A must also carve off portions of its Adult Social Care and Highways services to neighbouring Unitary C and Unitary D. The engine highlights the immediate need for Service Level Agreements (SLAs) to support the fragmented authorities.

nodes._note: CLEAN MERGE: Council A's Waste system is only going to the main merger partner (Unitary AB).
nodes.label: Bartec Waste

nodes._note: THE FRACTURE: Council A's Social Care system targets THREE future unitaries, but the database is Monolithic. The engine will flag this as a critical Disaggregation Risk.
nodes.label: Liquidlogic ASC (Monolith)

nodes._note: THE CLEAN FRACTURE: Highways is also splitting, but the data is easily segmented, so they can just export CSVs for C and D.
nodes.label: Confirm Highways

nodes._note: Main merger partner. Collides with Council A in Unitary AB.
nodes.label: Local Routing DB

nodes._note: Neighbouring authority receiving a fragment of Council A.
nodes.label: Mosaic ASC

nodes._note: Other neighbouring authority receiving a fragment.
nodes.label: CareDirector
